title: Record Schemas, Field Provenance, Confidence, and Data Quality · Type Forensics
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in loss-aware versioned site record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Version admitted records, field meanings, missingness, constraints, and compatible migrations independently from source markup.
Attach source URL, response identity, node or attribute path, raw value digest, transform version, time, and decision to each field.
Use confidence only when tied to observable rule evidence, calibration, thresholds, and review consequences.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Record Schema Version
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 9981 during an isolated browser, package, and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile versioned records, field provenance, confidence evidence, quality counts, and corrections; owner authorization and revocation must remain separate from robots and legal-review claims; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Version admitted records, field meanings, missingness, constraints, and compatible migrations independently from source markup.

Inspect the Record Schema Version trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Correction Deletion
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-correction-deletion-1bcewf" that uses case 2475 to reconcile versioned records, field provenance, confidence evidence, quality counts, and corrections. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Correction Deletion.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Correction Deletion into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Correction Deletion, keep Correction Deletion connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.
